core_mechanism: Self-dual (Bogomol'nyi/BPS) topological defect: minimization of a field-energy functional at the self-duality point where the energy saturates a lower bound fixed by a conserved integer topological charge (winding number), yielding stable localized soliton solutions of a spontaneously broken continuous symmetry, with an added conserved global current whose energy cost scales as the square of the total current.
brief_reason: The abstract constructs straight vortices in the Bogomol'ny limit of a model whose global SU(2) and local U(1) symmetries are spontaneously broken, with energy-minimizing solutions labeled by winding numbers and a global-current energy contribution proportional to the total current squared.
